## Project Objectives

1. Build a real-world personal finance dataset using CSV and simulate common data quality issues such as missing values and duplicate entries.
2. Load and inspect raw financial data using pandas to understand its structure, shape, and data types.
3. Clean the dataset by identifying and handling null values using median imputation and removing duplicate rows.
4. Convert and format data types correctly, including parsing date columns for time-series analysis.
5. Calculate monthly income, expense, and savings totals by grouping and aggregating data using pandas.
6. Derive a monthly savings rate percentage to measure financial discipline over time.
7. Perform a category-wise expense breakdown to identify the largest spending areas across the year.
8. Engineer a new running savings balance column to track cumulative financial growth month by month.
9. Visualize expense patterns using a bar chart grouped by spending category.
10. Represent savings versus total spending proportions using a pie chart.
11. Plot cumulative savings growth over 12 months using a line chart with annotations.
12. Combine all three visualizations into a single professional financial summary dashboard.
13. Generate an automated summary report in Markdown format with key metrics, findings, and recommendations — written as a client-facing bank document.
14. Demonstrate end-to-end data analysis skills — from raw CSV to cleaned data, analysis, visualization, and reporting — relevant to banking and financial services.
